page: Amazon Web Services r5.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by two Intel Xeon 8175M processors. It features 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the r5.metal demonstrates strong multi-core performance, achieving a stress-ng multi-core score of 81,870.14 and a PassMark CPU score of 57,080.9. Its memory subsystem shows high throughput, with cached read bandwidth reaching 4,545.56 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 222.69 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a PassMark database score of 21,974.43 and Redis SET performance of 74.16 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is moderate, and LLM text generation speeds are weak to poor, reflecting the older architecture's limitations in modern AI inference workloads.

As a bare-metal instance, r5.metal offers high resource density and eliminates virtualization overhead, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for large-scale, memory-bound enterprise applications. The absence of local storage requires external storage configuration, which represents a structural tradeoff. This server is highly suited for large in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, heavy software compilation tasks, and legacy enterprise workloads that require direct access to physical hardware and extensive RAM.
description: Amazon Web Services r5.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8175M processors at 3.1 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, offering a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks highlight strong multi-core capabilities, exceptional memory read bandwidth of up to 4,545.56 GB/sec, and high-performance database execution, including 74.16 million Redis SET operations per second. Conversely, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation is weak. This bare-metal configuration is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, intensive software compilation, and enterprise workloads requiring direct hardware access without hypervisor overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering high-throughput memory performance for enterprise databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs and 768 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and databases.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform featuring massive memory capacity and dedicated multi-core processing for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Bare-metal architecture with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 768 GB of DDR4 RAM.
High-throughput memory performance reaching 4,545.56 GB/sec cached read speed.
Strong multi-core execution suited for large-scale software compilation.
Optimized for in-memory databases with 74.16 million Redis SET operations per second.
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators.
